chemistry.scope_summary: Direct photodimerization of maleic anhydride using 400 W high-pressure Hg lamp with Pyrex filter in ethyl acetate solvent, performed in FEP microreactor with liquid/gas slug flow to prevent clogging from precipitated product

batch_baseline.vessel: cylindrical glass vessel (ID 75 mm; h 300 mm) covered with aluminum foil

optimization_table.key_findings: 10% MA concentration with ID = 0.8 mm tube gave 70% conversion at 5 min residence time

reactor.type: FEP tube wound around quartz beaker

light_source.type: high-pressure Hg lamp with Pyrex immersion well
light_source.position_relative_to_reactor: immersed in quartz beaker around which FEP tube is wound

figures.caption: Scheme 1.  Photodimerization of maleic anhydride (MA) to form cyclobutane tetracarboxylic dianhydride (CBTA)

figures.flow_path: plunger pump → needle valve → FEP tube in quartz beaker → FEP tube with segmented flow
figures.light_source_position: external to quartz beaker containing FEP tube
figures.key_information: Photochemical reactor with segmented flow using N2 and liquid segments, 400W mercury lamp irradiation, ultrasonic bath for mixing
figures.caption: Figure 1.  Single pass flow microreactor and continuous transportation of precipitates. | Figure 2.  Slug flow microreactor: (a) FEP tube reactor, wound around the outside of a quartz beaker; (b) N 2  gas feed, via T-shaped connector; (c) liquid/gas slug flow before and after irradiation. | Figure 3.  Continuous recycle flow microreactor with filter unit.

figures.flow_path: liquid and nitrogen gas merge at T-junction to create slug flow through microchannel
figures.key_information: liquid/gas slug flow system with before and after irradiation flow comparison showing effect of photochemical reaction
figures.caption: Figure 1.  Single pass flow microreactor and continuous transportation of precipitates. | Figure 2.  Slug flow microreactor: (a) FEP tube reactor, wound around the outside of a quartz beaker; (b) N 2  gas feed, via T-shaped connector; (c) liquid/gas slug flow before and after irradiation. | Figure 3.  Continuous recycle flow microreactor with filter unit.

figures.flow_path: plunger pump → filter unit → reactor with magnetic stirrer, with N2 inlet from top
figures.key_information: Setup shows a flow reactor with filtration, magnetic stirring, and nitrogen inlet capability
figures.caption: Figure 1.  Single pass flow microreactor and continuous transportation of precipitates. | Figure 2.  Slug flow microreactor: (a) FEP tube reactor, wound around the outside of a quartz beaker; (b) N 2  gas feed, via T-shaped connector; (c) liquid/gas slug flow before and after irradiation. | Figure 3.  Continuous recycle flow microreactor with filter unit.

Figure 4.  Change of conversion with various MA concentra- tions and residence time (tube OD  )  1.6 mm, ID  )  1.2 mm,  L )  11.2 m). | Figure 5.  Change of conversion with various tube sizes and residence time (MA concentration  )  10%). Inset shows tube size (OD (mm)  ×  ID (mm)  ×  length (m)). | Figure 6.  Change of conversion with increase of tube ID (MA concentration  )  10%, residence time  )  5 min). | Figure 7.  Solution color after irradiation reaction followed by filtration. After batch reaction for 6 h (left); after flow reaction for 10 min (right).

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Clogging from precipitated CBTA product in batch reactor
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Slug flow with N2 segmentation prevents clogging and enables continuous operation
translation_logic.what_changed: Reactor geometry from batch vessel to FEP microreactor with slug flow, addition of ultrasonication
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: UV light source (400W Hg lamp), solvent (ethyl acetate), photochemical mechanism
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Better temperature control at 15°C, continuous operation reduces handling

figure_descriptions: Mechanistic scheme showing MA → CBTA photodimerization
Multiple reactor configurations: single-pass, slug flow, and recycle flow systems
Optimization plots showing effects of concentration, tube size, and residence time

notes: Time reduction factor calculated as 360/5 = 72. Yield data not explicitly provided in extractions but conversion data available in optimization figures.
